comment.text: The "EnterpriseLicense" translation key is used to display the text "Enterprise License" in a UI component, likely as part of a licensing or upgrade process. It appears on-screen when users need to enter their Enterprise license information, and its purpose is to clearly identify the type of license being used for activation or upgrading.

usage.context:
<Text className={styles.text} fontWeight={600}>
 <Trans
 i18nKey="UpgradeToProBannerInformationAboutShort"
 ns="Common"
 t={t}
 values={{ license: t("Common:EnterpriseLicense") }}
 components={{
 1: (
 <Link
 key="enterprise-license-link"
 target={LinkTarget.blank}

<div className={styles.enterpriseComponent}>
 <Text fontWeight={700} fontSize="16px">
 {t("ActivateSwithToProHeader", {
 license: isDeveloper
 ? t("Common:DeveloperLicense")
 : t("Common:EnterpriseLicense"),
 })}
 </Text>
 
 <TariffTitleContainer
 isLicenseDateExpired={isLicenseDateExpired}

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The translation is in English instead of Arabic.
The Bulgarian translation is identical to the English text and uses the Latin script instead of the required Cyrillic script for the Bulgarian language.
The English term 'Enterprise' (referring to a business/corporate tier) is not translated into Finnish. Using the English word in a Finnish UI is considered an incorrect translation in this context.
The French translation 'Enterprise' is an English word. In French, the term for a business/corporate tier is 'Entreprise'.
The translation is in English instead of Armenian.

The translation uses the English word 'Enterprise' instead of the Korean equivalent. In a localized UI, technical terms like 'Enterprise' should be translated to the target language (e.g., '엔터프라이즈') unless specifically intended to remain in English.
The translation is in English (Latin script) instead of Lao script.
The Russian translation is provided in English (Latin script) instead of Russian (Cyrillic script).
The Sinhala translation is identical to the English text (Latin script) instead of using the Sinhala script.
The translation is in English instead of Serbian (Cyrillic).

The English term 'Enterprise' (referring to a business/corporate tier) is not translated into Turkish. Using the English word in a Turkish UI is considered an incorrect translation for a localized product.
The Ukrainian translation is identical to the English text, using the Latin script instead of the required Cyrillic script for the Ukrainian language.
The translation is identical to the English source, failing to translate the term into Vietnamese. In a software context, 'Enterprise' should be translated to convey the business/corporate tier.

The translation 'Ndërmarrja' means 'The Enterprise' (noun/entity), whereas in the context of 'Enterprise License', 'Enterprise' acts as an adjective describing the type of license. In Albanian UI, 'Enterprise' as a tier/category is better translated as 'Enterprise' (loanword) or 'Korporatë'. 'Ndërmarrja' sounds like a specific company name rather than a license tier.
